iteration_notes: Clean rewrite after QA rejection of iteration 2. All 5 NEW-B blockers and 5 NEW-M majors resolved. Codex iteration 3 consulted; 5 in-scope findings applied. AC numbering simplified: AC1-AC10 flat (no AC5a/AC5b split). AC5=warn verdict, AC6=push.sh absent, AC7=execv OSError. All mock push.sh tests use HOME isolation. --branch arg added throughout.

requirement.original: Create scripts/execute-push.py — an atomic push script that eliminates the timing window in the current /push Step 5 implementation. The &&-chained pattern (python3 validate_sentinel.py && bash push.sh) has a window between validator exit and push.sh start. os.execv eliminates this window. Update commands/push.md Step 5 to call this script directly.
requirement.clarified: Create scripts/execute-push.py with CLI args --repo-hash --branch --remote --request-id [--auto]; SESSION_ID from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var only; 13-step operation order (parse args, read SESSION_ID, compute grant path, check push.sh, read grant, parse JSON, validate non-head_sha fields, act on verdict, check HEAD drift at step 9 ONLY, consume grant, write sentinel, set env, execv). Update commands/push.md Steps 4+5 to single python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py invocation (tilde form) with --branch arg; Step 4 MUST NOT instruct orchestrator to manually unlink grant.
requirement.what: New script scripts/execute-push.py that atomically transitions from grant validation to push.sh execution within one process via os.execv
requirement.why: Eliminate timing window between validator exit and push.sh start that exists in && chaining; prevent subagent delegation of Steps 4+5 which causes both sentinel-write refusal (auth boundary) and dispatch latency exceeding TTL windows

included: commands/push.md Steps 4+5 — update to delegate grant validation/consume/sentinel-write/execv to the new script; remove && pattern, validate_sentinel.py reference, and direct bash push.sh

success_criteria: execute-push.py with valid grant (approved) + no HEAD drift writes Chain-B sentinel and exec()s push.sh as single process (AC1)
execute-push.py with verdict=warn displays risks[] to stderr, flushes, then exec()s push.sh (AC5)
execute-push.py exits 1 when grant is expired or any non-head_sha field mismatches, before sentinel is written, grant preserved (AC2)
execute-push.py exits 1 when grant.head_sha mismatches current HEAD at step 9 (single drift check only), before sentinel is written, grant preserved (AC3)
execute-push.py exits 1 when verdict=blocked; blocked grant is intentionally NOT consumed (AC4)
execute-push.py exits 2 when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset (AC8)
execute-push.py exits 2 when push.sh is absent; grant not consumed (AC6)
orphaned sentinel is cleaned up (exit 2) when os.execv raises OSError; grant was consumed at step 10 before sentinel write (AC7)
commands/push.md Step 4 states execute-push.py is sole handler (MUST NOT manually validate or unlink) (AC9)
commands/push.md Step 5 invokes python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py (tilde form) with --branch --request-id --repo-hash --remote args (AC10)
constraints: Sentinel schema must match exactly what hooks/push.sh:118-152 expects: exactly 5 fields — result, request_id, head, branch (raw), remote — all mandatory non-empty strings
SESSION_ID must come from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var, not CLI arg
Invocation path must be ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py (tilde-expanded), not /root/.claude/ form
--branch is a required CLI argument (not re-derived from git); used in grant field validation and sentinel
Single HEAD drift check at step 9 ONLY; no --pre-head CLI arg
Python 3.8+ compatible (expires_at Z-suffix parsing via replace('Z', '+00:00'))
os.execv, not subprocess.run — process image replacement is the core guarantee
Exit code taxonomy: exit 2 for setup/infrastructure failures (SESSION_ID unset, push.sh absent, execv OSError); exit 1 for grant validation failures
Flush stderr before os.execv so warn-path output is not buffered away
Test isolation: HOME=<tmpdir> for all tests needing mock push.sh; mock uses MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH env var; NEVER write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/push.sh (read-only: sentinel schema source of truth at lines 118-152, sentinel gate at lines 84-189)
out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: agents/push-analyst.md (grant schema defined here; not in scope)
settings.json (Skill(push:*) already denied; no change needed)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Skill(push:*) is already in the deny list at settings.json:253. No settings change needed for this cycle.

Test file coverage for execute-push.py was flagged by Codex as under-specified for a safety-critical flow. Codex proposed keeping production scope to scripts/execute-push.py + commands/push.md and optionally adding one focused test file. Test file creation was classified out_of_scope for this cycle; a separate cycle should add hooks/tests/test_execute_push.py or equivalent.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Live push invocation used python3 validate_sentinel.py && bash push.sh instead of the single-process exec pattern documented in commands/push.md Step 5
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: No dedicated Python script exists to enforce the exec pattern. The bash code block in commands/push.md Step 5 is illustrative; when orchestrators delegate Steps 4+5 to subagents, subagents either refuse the sentinel write (auth boundary) or accumulate dispatch latency that ages the grant/sentinel TTL windows. The spec describes exec but provides no enforcing artifact.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — gap is a missing artifact, not a regression commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The exec pattern was designed and documented (task 20260519-211515) but the corresponding Python script that enforces it was not created. The bash block in the spec is illustrative pseudocode, not an executable artifact.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The && pattern has three timing attack surfaces: sentinel mtime aging, grant replay window, and state substitution window. Subagent delegation adds dispatch latency that can exceed 60s sentinel mtime or 180s grant TTL. Both issues compound when Steps 4+5 are not atomically handled by a single orchestrator-level process.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced when commands/push.md was updated for task 20260519-211515 without a corresponding scripts/ artifact
observations: commands/push.md:211 shows exec pattern as documented intent (inferred from Read)
scripts/execute-push.py absent confirmed by codex exec: 'scripts/execute-push.py absent' (observed via codex bash invocation)
hooks/push.sh:110 shows 60s mtime check that is the binding constraint on sentinel freshness (inferred from Read)
hooks/push.sh:96 shows CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID is the request-id binding mechanism (inferred from Read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete — root cause is inferred from source reading; no runtime observation of the actual && invocation was made in this session (the post-mortem description is the only runtime evidence, provided by the orchestrator)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Orchestrator post-mortem report states live push used python3 validate_sentinel.py && bash push.sh. Codex confirmed scripts/execute-push.py absent via bash exec.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: commands/push.md:175-211 (Step 5 exec pattern documentation) and the absence of scripts/execute-push.py

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — this is a missing artifact, not a regression; no commit introduced a break

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: create scripts/execute-push.py as a single script
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Script exists at ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py and is executable

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Validates the push-analyst grant at the expected path (read, check all fields, expire check)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: All 9 grant fields validated per commands/push.md Step 4; any failure exits non-zero before sentinel write
Grant file is absent after execute-push.py run (on success path; after validation on abort path)
Sentinel appears atomically at correct path with all 5 required binding fields (result, request_id, head, branch, remote)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Checks for HEAD drift: git rev-parse HEAD == grant.head_sha (step 9 only, single check)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: HEAD drift at step 9 → exit 1, no sentinel written, grant preserved

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: exec()s push.sh (os.execv — replaces process, NOT subprocess) with CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID set
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: os.environ has CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID before execv; push.sh is exec'd not subprocess'd

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: commands/push.md Step 5 is updated to call this script directly (not delegate to subagent)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Step 5 in commands/push.md references python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py with all params from Step 2 snapshot

component_chain.role: Orchestrator dispatch spec — Steps 4+5 describe grant validation, consume, sentinel write, and exec; to be updated to delegate to execute-push.py
Atomic grant validator + sentinel writer + push.sh execv launcher
component_chain.evidence: Absence confirmed by codex bash exec; schema derived from hooks/push.sh:118-152 and commands/push.md:144-168

component_chain.role: Reads and consumes Chain-B sentinel at entry; aborts if missing/stale/FAIL/mismatched

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Rejects stale sentinel (mtime > 60s); this is the binding TTL constraint that execute-push.py's exec pattern must satisfy
Fallback chain for request_id; execute-push.py must set CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID to ensure request_id binding works correctly

setup.reason: non-UI — CLI; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). The push flow is a CLI security gate. Not a pipeline step.

evidence.observed: During the push (which succeeded) the implementation used: python3 validate_sentinel.py && bash push.sh — a two-process pattern with a timing window

measured.value: scripts/execute-push.py absent; commands/push.md:211 shows exec pattern as documented intent
measured.location: scripts/ directory (codex bash exec confirmation); commands/push.md:211

expected.value: os.execv call replacing Python process image with push.sh — sentinel write and push.sh sentinel-read in same PID lifetime
expected.source: commands/push.md:175-191 (exec pattern requirement); hooks/push.sh:84-189 (sentinel gate that must be satisfied)

evidence.gap: No scripts/execute-push.py file exists. The && pattern leaves a non-zero inter-process gap that can age the sentinel mtime, allow grant replay, and permit sentinel substitution.

additional_found: commands/push.md Step 4 grant-consume logic also needs updating (orchestrator must not manually unlink grant)

reference_source.description: Sentinel schema read from hooks/push.sh:118-152 (Python inline block); grant schema read from commands/push.md:144-168 — both verified in this session via Read tool

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts exist for this specific artifact creation

context.codebase_state: scripts/execute-push.py absent; commands/push.md documents exec pattern but provides no enforcing script
context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report; af96c61 fix(infra): M1-M4 gitignore exceptions

python_stdlib: subprocess (NOT for push.sh — for git rev-parse only)

development_approach.strategy: Create scripts/execute-push.py following the module pattern of scripts/write-commit-grant.py (argparse, typed main, named constants, docstring). Update commands/push.md Step 4 to remove any orchestrator-level unlink instruction; update Step 5 to replace any &&-chained invocation, validate_sentinel.py reference, or direct bash push.sh with the execute-push.py invocation pattern.
key_implementation_notes: 13-step operation order: (1) parse args, (2) read CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, (3) compute grant path, (4) check push.sh executable, (5) read grant file, (6) parse JSON, (7) validate non-head_sha fields, (8) act on verdict, (9) single HEAD drift check, (10) consume grant, (11) write sentinel atomically, (12) set CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID, (13) os.execv
Exit code taxonomy: exit 2 for setup/infrastructure errors (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset, push.sh absent, execv OSError); exit 1 for grant validation failures
Blocked verdict: do NOT consume grant; leave it on disk for audit (replay always re-blocks; new /push generates new request-id)
Warn verdict: display risks[] to stderr, flush stderr, then proceed to step 10 (consume+sentinel+execv)
os.execv argv: [push_sh_path, remote] + (['--auto'] if auto else []); push_sh_path is expanded ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh path
Flush sys.stderr before os.execv so warn-path output is not buffered away on process image replacement
Sentinel cleanup on execv OSError: unlink sentinel, then exit 2; grant WAS consumed at step 10 before sentinel write
Step 4 (push.sh check) must abort before step 10 (grant consume) so grant is preserved when push.sh is absent
Sentinel head field = grant.head_sha (proved equal to current HEAD by step 9; using grant field value avoids double git rev-parse)
HOME isolation for tests: mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh; script invoked by absolute path; MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH written by mock
development_approach.validation_approach: AC1-AC10: invoke execute-push.py by absolute path with HOME=<tmpdir> isolation for mock push.sh tests; fixture grant files written to /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/; assert sentinel contents, MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH contents, grant consumed/preserved per verdict, correct exit codes (2 vs 1). Grep-based AC9/AC10 verify commands/push.md changes.

_risk_level_doc: high because: (1) the script is in the critical security path for git push authorization; (2) a bug in sentinel write, grant consume, or execv handoff could either allow unauthorized pushes or permanently block push until manual cleanup; (3) the exec() pattern is unusual Python for most developers and has subtle semantics around env inheritance and argv.

iterations.feedback_summary: Codex identified 12 issues: 9 blockers/majors (missing step 4 scope update, path ambiguity, SESSION_ID as env not arg, wrong operation order writing sentinel before head-drift check, missing --auto flag, CLI args used as sole truth rather than cross-check, additional abort conditions without ACs, orphaned sentinel on execv raise, AC1 PID verification strategy) and 2 observations (test file coverage under-specified, settings.json expansion not needed). Terminology conflation between grant and sentinel also flagged.
iterations.feedback_incorporated: 9 blockers/majors applied: step 4 scope, invocation path, SESSION_ID from env, operation order corrected, --auto flag, CLI args as cross-check not sole trust, AC4 blocked verdict + AC5 execv failure, sentinel orphan cleanup, AC1 mock push.sh strategy, terminology distinction. 2 observations logged to out_of_scope_observations.

iterations.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed AC5a simulation correct; AC5b broken-shebang approach is sound; AC6 correctly isolates step 9; blocked grant unconsumed is safe. Three new in_scope_real_bug findings: (1) AC5a THEN must also assert grant still exists (abort before consume); (2) AC5b exit code must be 2 per exit-code taxonomy not 1; (3) stderr must be flushed before os.execv for warn-path correctness. One finding classified out_of_scope: no AC for os.execv vs subprocess proof (R6 already specifies; test quality gap for dev).
iterations.feedback_incorporated: 3 in_scope_real_bug findings applied: AC5a THEN adds grant_still_exists; AC5b exit code corrected to 2; operation order step 13 (stderr flush) added and M10 updated. 1 out_of_scope finding logged.

iterations.feedback_summary: Codex reviewed the clean iteration 3 draft (all NEW-B/NEW-M blockers resolved, --pre-head removed, single drift check at step 9). Five in-scope findings applied: (1) AC2 GIVEN must scope to non-head_sha mismatches only to avoid overlap with AC3; (2) AC6 (push.sh absent) and AC7 (execv OSError) must use HOME=<tmpdir> isolation; (3) --branch arg must be included in all AC invocation forms; (4) Q2 (AC7 isolation) resolved: use HOME=<tmpdir> with bad-shebang mock push.sh; (5) Q3 (sentinel head field) confirmed: use grant.head_sha (proved equal by step 9). Q1 (--branch vs HEAD branch): spec should note --branch comes from Step 2 snapshot in commands/push.md, not re-derived from git.

findings.issue: AC2 GIVEN does not scope to non-head_sha fields; could overlap with AC3
findings.proposed_fix: AC2 GIVEN explicitly states 'any non-head_sha mandatory field is wrong/missing/mismatched; head_sha is set to current HEAD so step 9 would pass'
findings.rationale: Without this scoping, a test with mismatched branch AND mismatched head_sha would satisfy both AC2 and AC3. The GIVEN must isolate which step fires.

findings.issue: AC6 and AC7 must also use HOME=<tmpdir> isolation (not write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh)
findings.proposed_fix: Both ACs: test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir>; AC6 does NOT create push.sh; AC7 creates push.sh with bad shebang at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh
findings.rationale: Same isolation contract as AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5. Mock must never write to live ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh (symlink to repo).

findings.issue: --branch arg missing from several AC invocation forms
findings.proposed_fix: All AC WHEN clauses: add --branch <branch> to the python3 execute-push.py invocation
findings.rationale: --branch is a required CLI arg per user constraints; omitting it causes argparse failure before any AC logic executes.

findings.issue: Q2: AC7 broken-shebang approach — HOME isolation or separate tmpdir path?
findings.proposed_fix: Use HOME=<tmpdir>; create <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh (chmod 0755) with shebang #!/tmp/execute-push-nointerp-<pid> (asserted non-existent); this causes os.execv OSError
findings.rationale: HOME isolation is cleaner; consistent with other ACs; avoids any ambiguity about which path the script resolves.

findings.issue: Q3: sentinel head field should use grant.head_sha not independently re-read git HEAD
findings.proposed_fix: Sentinel head = grant.head_sha (step 9 proved equality; using grant field avoids double git invocation and is semantically correct)
findings.rationale: Step 9 already proved grant.head_sha == current HEAD. Using grant.head_sha in the sentinel is correct and avoids a second subprocess call.

iterations.feedback_incorporated: 5 in-scope findings applied: AC2 GIVEN scoped to non-head_sha; AC6/AC7 HOME isolation; --branch added throughout; AC7 bad-shebang approach confirmed; sentinel head = grant.head_sha confirmed.
